cards.q: What two conditions stop the agent loop in run_agent?
cards.a: response.stop_reason is no longer "tool_use", or MAX_TURNS (12) is reached.
cards.hint: look at the for-loop and the if-break

cards.q: Why is `import anthropic` placed inside run_agent instead of at the top of agent.py?
cards.a: So run_agent_mock and --mock CLI usage work without the anthropic package installed.

cards.q: How does the agent loop send tool results back to Claude?
cards.a: It appends a user message containing a list of tool_result blocks, each keyed by tool_use_id.

cards.q: Why does get_weather seed its random generator with the city name?
cards.a: So repeated calls for the same city return identical forecasts, keeping demos reproducible.

cards.q: What do all four tool functions in travel_tools.py return?
cards.a: A JSON string, never a Python object and never a raised exception.

cards.q: How do tool functions report failures to the model?
cards.a: They catch exceptions and return {"error": ...} as JSON instead of raising.

cards.q: What three things does adding a new tool to this project require?
cards.a: A function, a schema entry in TOOL_SCHEMAS, and a registry entry in TOOL_FUNCTIONS.

cards.q: Compare search_flights and search_hotels: what do they share, and what's different?
cards.a: Both filter mock JSON by price and sort cheapest first; hotels also filter by min_stars, flights don't.

cards.q: Why must save_itinerary be called exactly once per trip, per the system prompt?
cards.a: To avoid duplicate itinerary files and force the model to finalize the plan before saving.

cards.q: What does MAX_TURNS protect the agent loop against?
cards.a: An unbounded loop if the model keeps requesting tools without ever finishing the plan.

cards.q: Compare run_agent and run_agent_mock: how do they decide which tools to call?
cards.a: run_agent lets Claude choose tool calls dynamically; run_agent_mock calls all four tools via fixed, scripted logic.
cards.hint: one asks a model, one doesn't

cards.q: Where does save_itinerary write its output, and how is the filename made unique?
cards.a: Into outputs/, as a markdown file named from the title plus a timestamp.

cards.q: Why does the system prompt instruct Atlas to never invent listings?
cards.a: Tools only know the mock JSON inventory; invented options would mislead the traveler with false data.
cards.hint: grounding rule near the end of prompts.py
